title: sambucus sieboldiana var. miquelii
intro: Sambucus sieboldiana var. miquelii, also known as Japanese elderberry, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to East Asia, characterized by its dark green leaves and clusters of small, fragrant white flowers that give way to black berries. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Japanese elderberry include reducing symptoms of the common cold and flu, relieving allergies and respiratory issues, and supporting immune function. Japanese elderberry can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in jams, wines, and other food products.
